abstract: This paper examines the 2021 German federal election and 2022 Australian federal election campaigns as they unfolded on social media platforms during the COVID-19 pandemic. No abstract or detailed text was available for this record, so the substantive content of the analysis cannot be summarised here. The work appears to be a comparative study produced by a multi-institutional team of digital media and political communication researchers.

methods: Methodological details are not available from the metadata provided; based on the authors' known research programs, the work likely involves social media data analysis of campaign-period activity across multiple platforms.
findings: Findings are not available from the metadata provided.
contributions: Offers a comparative perspective on two pandemic-era national election campaigns (Germany 2021 and Australia 2022) as conducted on social media, though specific contributions cannot be enumerated without the source text.
framing: Based on the title and author list, the paper sits within digital political communication and platform studies, examining how electoral campaigning operates on social media during the COVID-19 pandemic. It is positioned as a cross-national comparative study bridging German and Australian political communication scholarship.
